observe: Shortest job first. If you know how long something takes, run the quick one before the slow one — everyone waits less.
fault: You ran the long one out of habit. Two quick jobs just sat there for nothing.
repair: Reorder by burst time. Watch the average wait actually drop.
escape: Faster. Pick the short job before the Core picks for you.

observe: SJF scheduling selects the process with the shortest burst time next.
fault: A longer process was scheduled ahead of shorter ones.
escape: Complete the scheduling run before time expires.

hints: Sort strictly by burst time, shortest first, ignoring arrival order once all are present.
